subject: Computer Networking
title: Wireless Controllers
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Wireless Controllers.

hook.text: Managing one AP is easy. Managing 500 across ten buildings? You need a wireless controller.

buildup.text: A controller centrally manages config, firmware, channels, and power for every AP on the network.

discovery.text: It assigns non-overlapping channels automatically. Without this, APs interfere and kill performance.

twist.text: Cloud controllers like Meraki let IT teams manage global Wi-Fi from a browser. No on-site box needed.

climax.text: When an AP fails, the controller tells neighbors to boost power and cover the gap. Self-healing Wi-Fi.

punchline.text: A controller turns hundreds of APs into one seamless network.

quiz.question: What happens when an AP fails in a controller-managed network?
options: Neighboring APs boost power to cover the gap
All APs shut down and reboot
Users must manually switch to another AP
